mechanism_narrative: A rising trend in nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) reflects expanding aggregate income and consumption demand, which drives corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months when PAYEMS is in its top quartile of 12-month growth.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.25 percent, excess liquidity and near-zero borrowing costs inflate equity risk appetite, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days until liftoff expectations build.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed funds at 0.12% as of April 2015
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce negative 60-day forward returns in greater than 45% of occurrences, or average return is negative over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and industrial production (INDPRO) captures broad-based demand momentum that outpaces supply constraints, lifting profit margins and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and INDPRO month-over-month growth are positive, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative more than 40% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions with unemployment below 6%, an upward surprise in consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) raises the probability of imminent monetary tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered periods produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of instances, or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rising industrial production index (INDPRO) signals expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Top-decile INDPRO growth months are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns more than 50% of the time over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in housing starts (HOUST) below the 12-month rolling median signals weakening residential investment and downstream demand for construction materials and consumer durables, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST is below its 12-month median, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) and increases in payroll employment (PAYEMS) confirm a tightening labor market with job creation, boosting consumer spending forecasts and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint occurrences of falling UNRATE and rising PAYEMS are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns more than 40% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP (GDPC1) growth above 2.5 percent annualized confirms that aggregate demand is absorbing productive capacity, supporting corporate top-line beats and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth above 2.5% is followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45% of quarterly observations.
